content: Therapeutic ranges established from population studies correlating concentrations with efficacy and toxicity. Individual therapeutic targets may vary: warfarin INR 2-3 for most indications, 2.5-3.5 for mechanical mitral valve; digoxin 0.5-0.9 for heart failure (higher associated with mortality), 0.8-1.2 for atrial fibrillation. TDM interpretation requires understanding timing: steady state achieved after 4-5 half-lives; trough drawn immediately before next dose; peak timing drug-specific (gentamicin 30 min post-infusion, phenytoin 2-4 hours post-dose). Free drug monitoring for highly protein-bound drugs in special populations. Population pharmacokinetics uses Bayesian forecasting to individualize dosing based on sparse samples. Toxicity diagnosis: clinical signs plus supratherapeutic levels confirm; subtherapeutic levels with treatment failure indicate need for dose increase.
